The model favors Portugal at 51%, with Croatia the underdog at 20%. The remaining 30% probability centers on a draw, suggesting a competitive match where a stalemate result carries substantial likelihood.

The win-loss gap of 31 percentage points reflects a meaningful but not commanding edge for Portugal. A draw occupies the middle of the distribution, making the outcome genuinely three-sided rather than heavily tilted toward a single result. The contest appears likely to be closely contested in execution.

Model and sportsbook consensus align closely. Portugal sits at 51–52% across both sources, draws at 28–30%, and Croatia at 19–21%, with all gaps under two percentage points. The dashboard treats these as consistent signals. Polymarket per-match odds are not yet available.
